name: Rubik's Cube Coloring (easy version)
input: The first and only line contains the integers $k$ ($1 \le k \le 60$) — the number of levels in the perfect binary tree you need to color.
output: Print one integer — the number of the different colorings modulo $10^9+7$.
note: In the picture below, you can see one of the correct colorings of the first example.
